disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden onset (about 6 hours) of a harsh, seal-like barking cough and a hoarse/croaky voice, following two days of runny, blocked nose
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe the cough and the noise he's making?
How did this start, and was he unwell before it?
Has his voice changed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a really harsh, barking cough like a seal, and his voice has gone croaky. He'd had a runny, stuffy nose for a couple of days and then the barking started suddenly tonight.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Harsh noisy inspiration (squeak) is present ONLY when the child is crying or agitated, and settles completely when he is calm and at rest
trigger_question_examples: When exactly do you hear the squeaky breathing — all the time, or only when he's upset?
Is the noisy breathing there when he's calm and resting?
Does it settle when you cuddle him?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The squeaky noise is only when he cries or gets worked up. When we hold him and he calms down it goes away and he breathes quietly.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No harsh noisy breathing (stridor) heard when the child is completely calm and settled at rest
trigger_question_examples: When he is fully settled and quiet, can you still hear any noise as he breathes in?
Is there any squeak or harsh sound while he sleeps or rests calmly?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — when he's properly calm there's no squeak at all, he just breathes normally. It's only when he cries.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: He hasn't really been calm for long enough for me to tell.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Child is swallowing his own saliva and drinking sips, is not drooling, has no muffled 'hot potato' voice, and is not sitting hunched forward to breathe — features against epiglottitis
trigger_question_examples: Is he drooling or having trouble swallowing his spit?
Is he sitting leaning forward or refusing to lie back?
Does his voice sound muffled, like he has a hot potato in his mouth?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, he's swallowing fine and having little sips of water, he's not drooling, and he's not sitting forward — he'll still lie in my arms. His voice is croaky but not muffled.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't been watching his swallowing that closely.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Mild pulling-in of the skin around the ribs is seen only during crying and resolves completely at rest; no severe or sustained recession, tracheal tug or gasping when calm
trigger_question_examples: When he's calm, is the skin around his ribs or neck sucking in as he breathes?
Does he look like he's working hard to breathe when he's resting, or only when he's crying?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: When he cries you can see his ribs pull in a bit, but when he settles it stops and his breathing looks relaxed again.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Child is alert and interactive, lips and colour look normal (not pale or blue), and he is not floppy or drowsy
trigger_question_examples: What colour are his lips and face — any blue or grey tinge?
Is he alert and looking around, or unusually sleepy and hard to rouse?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's alert and looking around at us, his colour looks normal, no blue lips or anything, and he's definitely not floppy — he's cross and wriggly.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Only a low-grade temperature (~37.8 C); child is not described as high-fever toxic, floppy or severely unwell
trigger_question_examples: Does he have a temperature, and how high?
Apart from the cough, how is he in himself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He feels a bit warm — about 37.8 under the arm — but he's not burning up, and between the coughing fits he's his usual cheeky self.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms are markedly worse at night and worsening since nightfall; a steamy bathroom gave only minimal relief
trigger_question_examples: Is it worse at any particular time?
Have you tried anything, and did it help?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's much worse at night — it's really ramped up since it got dark. We sat him in a steamy bathroom and it only helped a tiny bit.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Only a single sub-therapeutic dose of paracetamol given ~2 hours ago; no other medication and no regular medicines
trigger_question_examples: Have you given him anything for it, and how much?
Is he on any regular medicines?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Just a small dose of children's Panadol about two hours ago — I wasn't sure how much to give. Nothing else, and he's not on anything regular.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No sudden choking episode while eating or playing, and no history of a small object being swallowed or inhaled before the illness — argues against inhaled foreign body
trigger_question_examples: Was there any moment he suddenly choked on food or a small toy before this started?
Could he have put anything small in his mouth just before the cough began?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, there was no choking or anything like that — it came on after his cold, not out of the blue while he was eating or playing.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Does he need to go to hospital in an ambulance? The noise is so scary.
Is there some medicine that will make the swelling go down quickly?
